question_text: Why does car tyre pressure increase after driving on a hot day?

options.label: A
options.text: The volume of the tyre increases

options.label: B
options.text: The gas inside heats up, causing particles to move faster

options.label: C
options.text: More air leaks into the tyre

options.label: D
options.text: The rubber expands and pushes the gas out

correct_answer: The gas inside heats up, causing particles to move faster
explanation: When a car tyre heats up from friction during driving, the gas particles inside gain kinetic energy and move faster. These faster-moving particles collide with the tyre walls more frequently and with greater force, which increases the pressure inside the tyre. This is an application of Gay-Lussac's Law (pressure increases with temperature at constant volume).
